scope: Close M3-CF-01 carry-forward from vuln-migration-v1 (32 of 33 currently-RED positive tests across 15 languages). Empirical investigation reframes the carry-forward from 'source-bank parity' to 'source-AND-sink parity': of 33 RED tests, ~14 are sink-bank gaps, 4 are source-qualifier gaps (CPP std::*), 4 are combined source+sink gaps (Swift Vapor), 2 are reclassifications (res.send: FileWrite→HtmlOutput), 2 are fixture-shape issues (eval-as-deser in JS/TS), 4 are Rust-specific source-extraction issues (M1 will categorize as additive-fix vs carry-forward), 1 is a Ruby backtick-subshell carry-forward exception (mirrors FAI-v1 \bgets\b precedent). NO public API changes, NO new TaintSourceType/TaintSinkType/VulnType enum variants, NO engine refactor — purely additive bank entries plus one in-place sink_type field flip for the res.send family.
closes_carry_forward: vuln-migration-v1 M3-CF-01 (positive-fixture coverage gaps for several (lang, vuln_type) pairs) and M4-CF-01 (python_xss_positive RED — same class).
enables_followons: future taint-extraction-v1 (if Rust source-extraction surfaces as carry-forward in M1)

validator_mandates.red_first_harness_required: true — vuln_migration_v1_red.rs is the existing RED contract from vuln-migration-v1 M1; this milestone authors NO new tests. The existing 33 RED + 133 GREEN tests are the binary-verifiable success contract. M5 stop-threshold reports 165/166 GREEN (1 retained RED is the Ruby backtick carry-forward).
validator_mandates.additive_bank_change_only: M2 must be PURELY ADDITIVE on source/sink banks (no removal of existing entries). The executor's M2 verification report MUST diff the bank arrays before/after and enumerate added entries explicitly. The single non-additive change is M3's reclassification of (res, send), (reply, send), (response, send), (Response, send) sink_type field from TaintSinkType::FileWrite to TaintSinkType::HtmlOutput — that lands in M3 with explicit pre-flight grep verification.
validator_mandates.string_literal_regression_zero_new_fps: All 83 string-literal regression-guard tests in vuln_migration_v1_red.rs MUST remain GREEN at every milestone gate. M2/M3/M4 stop-thresholds gate on this. Closing-#24 root pattern is non-negotiable.
validator_mandates.e2e_test_preservation_mandatory: All 36 test_e2e_* tests at tldr-core/security/vuln.rs:1568-2100 MUST remain GREEN. Primary regression guard from vuln-migration-v1.
validator_mandates.m3_reclassification_pre_flight_grep_requ: Before M3 lands, executor runs `grep -rn 'res\.send' crates/tldr-cli/tests/ crates/tldr-core/tests/ | grep -i path_traversal` (and variants for reply.send / response.send / Response.send) and includes the result in M3-report.json. If the grep returns hits OTHER than the failing javascript_xss_positive / typescript_xss_positive themselves, M3 must pause and triage.
validator_mandates.rust_disposition_explicit: M1 deliverable is a per-test categorical decision for each of the 4 Rust RED tests: (a) additive-bank-resolvable (route to M2), (b) source-extraction-bug-not-additive-fixable (route to carry-forward), or (c) fixture-shape-issue (route to M4 fixture-fix). Aggregate post-M5 carry-forward count cannot exceed 5 (Ruby backtick + Rust 4 if all (b)).
validator_mandates.carry_forward_exceptions_documented: Any test that does NOT transition GREEN appears in reports/M5-carry-forward.json with predecessor-precedent reference. Ruby backtick carry-forward references FAI-v1's \bgets\b precedent verbatim — same shape (AST cannot structurally express without FP risk).
validator_mandates.obsolete_test_count_is_zero: This milestone authors no new tests AND deletes no existing tests. The vuln_migration_v1_red.rs harness is the contract; no obsolete test enumeration required.
validator_mandates.fixture_fix_atomic: M4 ships in ONE commit: fixture rewrites for js/ts deserialization_positive PLUS the corresponding TYPESCRIPT_AST_SINKS bank addition (node-serialize unserialize). Splitting these would leave the test suite inconsistent (the rewritten fixture would reference an un-wired sink).
validator_mandates.no_public_api_change: compute_taint_with_tree, scan_vulnerabilities, detect_sources_ast, detect_sinks_ast — all signatures unchanged. No new exports. Bank arrays are crate-internal `static` items; modifying them is not a public API change.
validator_mandates.no_enum_variant_extension: TaintSourceType, TaintSinkType, VulnType — no new variants. If M1 surfaces a need for a new variant (e.g., a hypothetical TaintSourceType::WebSocketMessage), that is OUT OF SCOPE for this milestone and routes to a separate enum-extension milestone.
validator_mandates.no_perf_regression: The structural perf carry-forward M3-CF-02 (avg 17.18× M1, p99 5.24× M1) is documented but not addressed here. M5 verification reports same-order perf as HEAD (no NEW regression introduced by adding bank entries; bank lookup is O(N_patterns) per descendant, and the additions are <100 entries total across 16 banks).
validator_mandates.carry_forward_max_5: Ruby backtick (1) + worst-case Rust (4 if all categorized as type-b in M1) = 5. If M1 surfaces additional carry-forward beyond Rust (e.g., a Java FQN issue that turns out to be a tree-sitter parse-shape bug), M5 stop-threshold MUST surface it explicitly with a non-additive-resolution rationale; the executor does NOT extend scope to fix engine-level issues.
validator_mandates.m3_test_reclassification_atomic: M3 bank flip (res.send/reply.send FileWrite→HtmlOutput in TYPESCRIPT_AST_SINKS) MUST atomically include the 2 test assertion updates at rr_framework_integ_test.rs:157-177 (fastify_reply_send_reflected_via_compute_taint, assertion at line 168 `TaintSinkType::FileWrite` → `TaintSinkType::HtmlOutput`) and :237-257 (nestjs_res_send_reflected_via_compute_taint, assertion at line 248 analogous). Both tests assert the sink_type variant DIRECTLY via matches!, NOT the projected vuln_type — the plan's previous pre-flight grep convention (`res\.send.*path_traversal`) was filter-bugged and would have falsely confirmed safety. Non-atomic ship breaks the regression guard. Per premortem E1 (BLOCKER).
validator_mandates.m2_m3_m4_serial: M2, M3, M4 ALL edit crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs at non-overlapping ranges (per-language AST source/sink banks). M3 and M4 both edit TYPESCRIPT_AST_SINKS specifically. Naive parallelisation would race on the same file. Executor MUST serialize: M2 → M3 → M4 under a single executor session. Mirrors the FAI-v1 + sanitizer-v1 + vuln-migration-v1 precedent. Per premortem E2 (MEDIUM).

staging_method.verification: git show HEAD --stat | awk '/continuum\/autonomous\// { print }' | grep -v 'vuln-source-parity-v1-plan/' && echo LEAK || echo STAGING-OK
staging_method.verification_note: Per premortem RM-4: the prior `grep -E 'continuum/autonomous/(?!vuln-source-parity-v1-plan/)'` regex used PCRE negative lookahead which is unsupported by macOS BSD grep -E and would have been a no-op. Replaced with portable awk + grep -v pipeline. Manual `git show HEAD --stat` audit remains canonical regardless.
staging_method.rationale: Prevents accidental staging of unrelated continuum/autonomous artifacts (especially the existing unstaged M5/M6 SHA-backref edits in vuln-migration-v1-plan/, FAI-v1-plan/, sanitizer-removal-v1-plan/, etc.). Cargo.lock NEVER staged — `git checkout HEAD -- Cargo.lock` before each commit if dirty.

milestones.title: Investigate Rust source-extraction + lock per-test RED disposition
milestones.scope: Run all 33 RED fixtures through the post-M4 binary; for each, categorize the failure mode: (S) source missing, (K) sink missing, (B) both missing, (T) wrong vuln_type (sink wired to wrong TaintSinkType variant), (F) fixture-shape mismatch with canonical taxonomy, (R) Rust .unwrap chain source-extraction issue, (X) AST inexpressible carry-forward. For Rust 4 tests specifically, decide additive-fix vs carry-forward. Emit reports/M1-investigation.json with per-test category + per-test root-cause prose. Optionally land a 0-30 LOC source-extraction fix in taint.rs IF M1 finds an additive resolution for Rust (e.g., extend extract_first_identifier_arg_ast to descend through .unwrap chains).

The plan's prior 'parallel after M1' framing was incorrect for taint.rs file-overlap. Executor MUST serialize: M2 → M3 → M4 under a single executor session. Per validator_mandate m2_m3_m4_serial.
milestones.scope: Apply the additions enumerated in plan.md §3 across taint.rs source/sink banks: C/Cpp SqlQuery banks; Cpp std::getenv source qualifier + std::fopen sink qualifier; CSharp Response.Write HtmlOutput + Process.Start FQN + System.IO.File.Open FQN + JavaScriptSerializer/XmlSerializer/SoapFormatter Deserialize; Elixir bang-convention SqlQuery/FileOpen + :os.cmd/System.shell/Port.open ShellExec; Java new java.io.File / new java.io.ObjectInputStream FQN; Lua/Luau :query( colon-method SqlQuery; OCaml Mariadb.Stmt.execute / Postgresql.exec / Mysql.exec / Sqlite3.prepare SqlQuery; Python response.write / Response.set_data HtmlOutput; Ruby SqlQuery NEW BANK (ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute, raw_sql); Scala scala.io.Source.fromFile / new java.io.ObjectInputStream FQN; Swift Vapor request.query[ HttpParam source + executeQuery/prepareStatement SqlQuery + Process.launchedProcess/Process.run ShellExec + FileHandle(forReadingAtPath: FileOpen.

0 currently-GREEN positive tests transition RED.

milestones.title: Reclassify res.send family — FileWrite → HtmlOutput (ATOMIC with test edits)
milestones.serialization_note: Depends on M2 (not just M1) because M2/M3/M4 ALL edit taint.rs and M3+M4 both edit TYPESCRIPT_AST_SINKS — must serialize to avoid merge conflicts under any parallel-agent harness. Per validator_mandate m2_m3_m4_serial.
milestones.scope: In TYPESCRIPT_AST_SINKS at taint.rs:2003 (W1-M3 NestJS block), MOVE the four entries (res, send), (response, send), (response, redirect — KEEP as FileWrite), (response, json — KEEP as FileWrite), (Response, send) from sink_type: FileWrite to a NEW HtmlOutput AstSinkPattern entry. Also move (reply, send) from the W1-M2 Fastify block at taint.rs:1981 to HtmlOutput. The redirect/json variants stay FileWrite (semantically navigation/JSON-emit, not Xss). Update the inline comment at taint.rs:2020-2028 to retire the M2 deferred-reclassification carry-forward note. ATOMIC: this commit MUST also include the 2 test assertion updates at crates/tldr-core/tests/rr_framework_integ_test.rs:157-177 (fastify_reply_send_reflected_via_compute_taint — line 168 changes `TaintSinkType::FileWrite` → `TaintSinkType::HtmlOutput`) and :237-257 (nestjs_res_send_reflected_via_compute_taint — line 248 analogous). Both currently-GREEN tests assert the sink_type variant DIRECTLY via matches!, NOT the projected vuln_type. Non-atomic ship would flip them RED and break the regression guard. The plan's previous pre-flight grep convention (res\.send.*path_traversal) is filter-bugged and would have missed these — premortem E1 surfaced them via a corrected grep on `TaintSinkType::FileWrite` near `send`.
files_modified: crates/tldr-core/tests/rr_framework_integ_test.rs (UPDATE 2 test assertions atomic with M3 bank flip — line 157-177 fastify_reply_send_reflected_via_compute_taint at line 168 changes `TaintSinkType::FileWrite` → `TaintSinkType::HtmlOutput`; line 237-257 nestjs_res_send_reflected_via_compute_taint at line 248 analogous. Both edits ship in the SAME commit as the M3 bank flip — non-atomic would break the regression guard.)

milestones.title: Fixture fix for js/ts deserialization (atomic with bank addition)
milestones.serialization_note: Depends on M3 (not just M1) because M3 and M4 BOTH edit TYPESCRIPT_AST_SINKS in taint.rs (M3 reclassifies res.send/reply.send entries; M4 adds the Deserialize bank entry). Naive parallelization would race on the same file region. Executor MUST serialize: M2 → M3 → M4 under a single executor session. Per validator_mandate m2_m3_m4_serial.
milestones.scope: Rewrite fixtures javascript/deserialization_positive.js + typescript/deserialization_positive.ts to exercise a real Deserialize sink (e.g., `const serialize = require('node-serialize'); serialize.unserialize(d);` for JS / TS-equivalent). Concurrently add the corresponding TYPESCRIPT_AST_SINKS Deserialize bank entry: { call_names: &["unserialize"], member_patterns: &[("", "node-serialize")], sink_type: TaintSinkType::Deserialize }. ATOMIC because splitting these into two commits leaves the suite inconsistent: rewrite-then-bank-add intermediate commit has the fixture asserting vuln_type=deserialization but the engine has no matching sink, so the test stays RED until the second commit; bank-add-then-rewrite intermediate commit adds an unused bank entry (clippy/dead_code clean) but is harmless. The atomic-commit gate prevents accidental scope-leak (e.g., the rewriter forgetting to update the FP fixture).

milestones.title: CHANGELOG entry + local tag
milestones.scope: Add CHANGELOG entry per plan.md §6. Apply local tag `vuln-source-parity-v1` (no push, no publish). Optionally add a brief release-prep note documenting that this milestone closes M3-CF-01 and bumps the M1-RED test pass rate from 134/166 (post-M4) to 165/166 (post-M5).

rollback_plan.if_M2_fails: git revert <M2_sha>. Bank additions are pure additions; reverting drops them cleanly. No downstream impact on test_e2e_* or string-literal regression-guards.
rollback_plan.if_M3_breaks_green_test: git revert <M3_sha>. The reclassification flips back to FileWrite. javascript_xss_positive / typescript_xss_positive return to RED state; no other tests affected.
rollback_plan.if_M4_breaks_string_literal_fp: git revert <M4_sha>. Atomic commit means revert is single-step. Fixtures revert to eval(d); bank entry for unserialize is dropped; the 2 RED tests return to RED state.
rollback_plan.if_M5_surfaces_unanticipated_carry_forwa: Document in M5-carry-forward.json with rationale; do NOT extend milestone scope. Aggregate carry-forward count check (<=5) flags the executor to pause and triage IF count exceeds bound.

success_summary_post_m6.carry_forward_aggregated: 1 (Ruby backtick) — maximum 5 if Rust 4 categorized as type-(b).

post_publish_unblock: After M6 lands, the v0.2.x release group (vuln-migration-v1 + vuln-source-parity-v1 + workspace-test-infrastructure-v1) is publish-ready. The published binary's `tldr vuln` will detect 32 (lang, vuln_type) positive cases that the post-M4 binary silently missed.
